name: Crude Oil Futures (WTI)
summary: PLACEHOLDER ASSESSMENT. The numeric score, price levels and positioning figures below are illustrative placeholders, not live readings — replace them after reviewing current EIA/OPEC/IEA data and CFTC positioning. Structurally, crude sentiment is a tug-of-war between supply-side risk premium (OPEC+ policy, geopolitics, inventory draws) and demand-side caution (China growth, a firm USD, and global growth signals). With placeholder inputs the framework leans mildly bullish, but confidence is Low because no live data has been wired in for this run.
macroContext: Crude is highly sensitive to the U.S. dollar and real rates (a stronger USD and higher real yields are typically headwinds), to global growth expectations, and to China — the largest marginal importer. Watch DXY, the front-end of the U.S. curve, and China PMIs/imports. OBSERVED vs INFERRED: the directional sensitivities are observed/structural; the current magnitude is inferred and unquantified in this placeholder run.
newsContext: Energy newsflow is dominated by OPEC+ production decisions, geopolitical supply disruptions (Middle East, Russia sanctions, shipping/Strait risk), refinery outages and weekly inventory headlines. No single headline should drive the read — look for convergence across EIA data, OPEC/IEA reports and multiple wires.
socialContext: LIVE (StockTwits ($CL_F), as of 2026-06-22 03:51 UTC): 8 bullish / 14 bearish (36% bullish, 9 natively tagged) across 30 recent messages. Treat retail chatter as a contextual/contrarian signal, not a standalone one.
whatWouldChange: HIGHER: surprise OPEC+ cuts or extended quotas, sustained large inventory draws, a credible supply disruption, a weaker USD, or improving China demand. LOWER: OPEC+ unwinding cuts/raising output, persistent inventory builds, demand downgrades from EIA/IEA, a stronger USD/higher real rates, or a global growth scare.

subScores.name: Supply outlook
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: OPEC+ retains spare capacity and non-OPEC (US/Brazil/Guyana) supply has been resilient. INFERRED: modest net support pending the latest STEO/OMR balances. Update with current EIA STEO supply figures.

subScores.name: Demand outlook
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: China demand growth has been the key swing variable and global-growth signals are mixed. INFERRED: slight drag. Update with EIA/IEA demand revisions and China import data.

subScores.name: Inventory trend
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. Weekly EIA crude + product stocks and Cushing levels drive near-term price. INFERRED a draw-leaning bias here; replace with the latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report.

subScores.name: OPEC+ / geopolitical risk
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: an active geopolitical risk premium (Middle East, sanctions, shipping) and OPEC+ policy optionality skew tail risks to the upside. Magnitude unquantified — update around OPEC+ meetings and live events.

subScores.name: USD / rates pressure
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED structural inverse link to USD and real rates. INFERRED a modest headwind. Update with current DXY and real-yield levels.

subScores.name: Positioning
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. CFTC managed-money net length and producer hedging matter but EXACT NUMBERS ARE NOT AVAILABLE in this run. Update from the weekly CFTC COT report; treat as unavailable until then.

subScores.name: Technical trend
subScores.reasoning: OBSERVED (live): price < 50-DMA, price > 200-DMA, golden cross (50>200), RSI 30 soft. Auto-computed from Yahoo Finance daily data.

subScores.name: News/social sentiment
subScores.reasoning: OBSERVED (live): 8 bullish / 14 bearish across recent StockTwits messages (36% bullish, 9 natively tagged). Auto-computed; small samples are dampened.

subScores.name: Volatility / risk premium
subScores.reasoning: OBSERVED (live): ~47% annualized realized vol (20d). Lower vol = steadier risk backdrop; higher vol = more two-way risk. Auto-computed from Yahoo Finance.

subScores.name: Cross-market confirmation
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. Cross-checks vs energy equities (XLE), Brent–WTI spread and broad commodities. INFERRED slightly non-confirming. Update with live cross-asset reads.

drivers.evidence: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report drives near-term price; direction depends on the latest print (PLACEHOLDER — update weekly).
Quota decisions and spare-capacity guidance set the supply baseline. Direction depends on the latest meeting outcome.
Middle East tensions, Russia sanctions and shipping/Strait-of-Hormuz risk add an upside tail premium.
China is the largest marginal importer; import volumes and stimulus drive the demand read.
A firmer USD and higher real yields are structural headwinds for dollar-priced crude.
Resilient US shale, Brazil and Guyana output can cap rallies; see EIA STEO supply balances.
Crack spreads and maintenance season influence refiner crude demand and product pull.
Crowded net length raises pullback risk; light positioning leaves upside fuel. EXACT FIGURES UNAVAILABLE — update from CFTC COT.

risks.risk: OPEC+ unwinds cuts faster than expected
risks.description: An accelerated return of barrels would loosen balances and pressure prices.
Weaker-than-expected Chinese imports/stimulus removes a key pillar of demand.
A reduction in supply-disruption risk would deflate the risk premium.
Tighter financial conditions and a firm dollar weigh on commodities.
An unexpected outage or escalation could spike prices against a short-leaning view.

narratives.name: Tight supply risk from geopolitics
narratives.summary: An ongoing geopolitical risk premium (Middle East, sanctions, shipping) keeps a floor under crude.
narratives.evidence: Headline-driven spikes; backwardated curve at times; elevated freight/insurance.
narratives.confirmation: Actual supply disruptions, falling exports from a key producer, widening backwardation.
narratives.invalidation: Sustained de-escalation, no real barrels lost, premium bleeds out.

narratives.name: Demand concern from China slowdown
narratives.summary: Soft Chinese growth and property weakness cap the demand outlook.
narratives.evidence: Mixed China PMIs, uneven import volumes, IEA/EIA demand revisions.
narratives.confirmation: Falling Chinese crude imports, downgraded demand forecasts.
narratives.invalidation: Stimulus-driven rebound, stronger imports, upgraded demand.

narratives.name: Inventory draws supporting near-term prices
narratives.summary: Sustained stock draws (incl. Cushing) signal a tightening physical market.
narratives.evidence: Consecutive EIA crude/product draws (PLACEHOLDER — verify weekly).
narratives.confirmation: Multi-week draws, falling Cushing, firm prompt spreads.
narratives.invalidation: Persistent builds, rising Cushing, weakening prompt structure.

narratives.name: OPEC+ spare-capacity uncertainty
narratives.summary: Debate over true spare capacity and policy intent creates two-way risk.
narratives.evidence: Conflicting guidance on quota unwinds vs extensions.
narratives.confirmation: Clear quota policy direction at the next meeting.

narratives.name: Stronger USD pressuring commodities
narratives.summary: A firm dollar and higher real rates weigh on dollar-priced crude.
narratives.evidence: DXY strength alongside hawkish Fed pricing.
narratives.confirmation: Rising DXY/real yields with crude underperforming.

positioning.summary: Framework: monitor CFTC managed-money net length (crowding risk), producer/merchant hedging, options skew (calls vs puts), and energy-equity/ETF flows (e.g. XLE, USO). Crowded longs raise pullback risk; light positioning leaves room to rally.
positioning.limitations: Exact CFTC Commitments of Traders, options-skew and ETF-flow numbers are NOT available in this run. Update from the weekly CFTC COT report and exchange/ETF data, then set available=true.

catalysts.event: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report (crude inventories)
catalysts.whyItMatters: Weekly stock changes drive near-term supply/demand balance and price.
Previews the EIA report; can move price after the close.
Sets the supply baseline via quotas and spare-capacity guidance.
Update global supply/demand balances and forecasts.
Can add or remove a risk premium quickly.

confirmationSignals: Break and hold above resistance (PLACEHOLDER level)
Break and hold below support (PLACEHOLDER level)

rangeConditions: Risk premium neither building nor bleeding
Price oscillating between PLACEHOLDER support and resistance
rangeBreakSignals: Decisive close beyond the range with follow-through volume
Step-change in OPEC+ policy or geopolitics

sources.title: Monthly Oil Market Report & meeting schedule

samples.body:
$CL_F 

Looks like Trump won't even need to jawbone this at 7 AM. 

Life is good as an oil bear.
$CL_F US is being run by idiots, they  are clueless and don't know how to make a deal.  Too many ppl r short this which is gonna add oil to the fire lol

$USO $SPY $XLE $CL_F  
 
WTI up a buck at the open. Let the BS games begin.  
.
$USO $CL_F $SPY $DJT guarantee you Trump says something that causes a deal to be put in place. Wont be any escalation. It's all bluffs.

name: S&P 500 E-mini Futures
summary: PLACEHOLDER ASSESSMENT. The numeric score, index levels and positioning figures below are illustrative placeholders, not live readings — replace them after reviewing current earnings trends, Fed/rates pricing, inflation/labor data, breadth and volatility. Structurally, ES sentiment hinges on the interplay of earnings resilience and the rates/Fed backdrop, with mega-cap/AI leadership, breadth and liquidity as key swing factors. With placeholder inputs the framework reads roughly neutral; confidence is Low because no live data has been wired in.
macroContext: Equity-index sentiment is driven by the rates/discount-rate regime (Fed policy, real yields, the 10Y), the inflation path (CPI/PCE), and growth/labor data (NFP, ISM, GDP, jobless claims). Tighter financial conditions compress multiples; easing supports them. Watch the 2Y/10Y, financial-conditions indices and credit spreads.
newsContext: Newsflow centers on Fed communication, the inflation/labor data cycle, mega-cap earnings (especially AI capex narratives), and credit/liquidity headlines. Avoid over-weighting any single print or pundit — confirm across the data calendar and multiple outlets.
socialContext: LIVE (StockTwits ($ES_F, $SPY, $SPX), as of 2026-06-22 03:51 UTC): 30 bullish / 21 bearish (59% bullish, 21 natively tagged) across 90 recent messages. Treat retail chatter as a contextual/contrarian signal, not a standalone one.
whatWouldChange: HIGHER: dovish Fed repricing / falling yields, cooling inflation with intact growth, broadening breadth, resilient mega-cap earnings, easing financial conditions, tight credit spreads. LOWER: hawkish repricing / rising real yields, inflation reacceleration, labor or growth deterioration, narrowing breadth, widening credit spreads, a volatility/liquidity shock.

subScores.name: Earnings outlook
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: index EPS has been carried by mega-cap/AI names. INFERRED modest support. Update with the latest earnings-season beat rates and forward-EPS revisions.

subScores.name: Rates / Fed policy
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: policy/real-rate regime is the dominant macro driver. INFERRED a mild headwind pending live FOMC pricing. Update with current FOMC guidance and futures-implied path.

subScores.name: Inflation trend
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: disinflation vs reacceleration risk is pivotal for multiples. INFERRED slightly supportive. Update with the latest CPI/PCE prints.

subScores.name: Growth / labor data
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: soft-landing vs slowdown debate. INFERRED mildly positive. Update with NFP, jobless claims, ISM and GDP.

subScores.name: Market breadth
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: leadership has at times been narrow (mega-cap concentration). INFERRED a drag/fragility flag. Update with advance/decline, % above 200-DMA, equal- vs cap-weighted spread.

subScores.name: Volatility / credit conditions
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: low VIX + tight credit spreads = risk-on; spikes/widening = risk-off. INFERRED mildly benign. Update with VIX and IG/HY spreads.

subScores.name: Positioning
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. CFTC futures positioning, dealer gamma, put/call and systematic (CTA/vol-target) exposure matter, but EXACT FIGURES ARE NOT AVAILABLE. Update from CFTC COT and options data; treat as unavailable until then.

subScores.name: Technical trend
subScores.reasoning: OBSERVED (live): price > 50-DMA, price > 200-DMA, golden cross (50>200), RSI 56 firm. Auto-computed from Yahoo Finance daily data.

subScores.name: News/social sentiment
subScores.reasoning: OBSERVED (live): 30 bullish / 21 bearish across recent StockTwits messages (59% bullish, 21 natively tagged). Auto-computed; small samples are dampened.

subScores.name: Liquidity / flows
subScores.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. OBSERVED: financial conditions, reserves/liquidity and fund flows shape the bid. INFERRED mildly supportive. Update with FCI, fund-flow and liquidity proxies.

drivers.evidence: The discount-rate regime is the dominant macro driver of index multiples; direction depends on the latest FOMC guidance and implied path.
Disinflation supports multiples; reacceleration is a key downside risk. Update with the latest releases.
A handful of mega-caps drive a large share of index EPS and returns; AI-capex narrative is central.
Narrow leadership raises fragility; broadening participation is healthier. Update with A/D, % above 200-DMA.
NFP, jobless claims, ISM and GDP define the soft-landing vs slowdown read.
The 10Y and real yields move the discount rate; auctions and term premium matter.
Tight IG/HY spreads = risk-on; widening warns of stress.
Low VIX supports carry/risk-taking; spikes coincide with drawdowns.
Easier financial conditions and ample liquidity support the bid; tightening pressures it.

risks.description: A hotter CPI/PCE path forces hawkish repricing and pressures multiples.
Rising real yields compress valuations, especially for long-duration growth names.
Concentration means a few names falling can drag the index disproportionately.
Stress in credit or funding markets typically coincides with equity drawdowns.
A sharper slowdown undermines the soft-landing earnings narrative.
Falling yields with intact growth would support a melt-up against a bearish view.

narratives.name: Soft landing / earnings resilience
narratives.summary: Growth holds up and earnings keep beating, supporting the index.
narratives.evidence: Resilient labor data, positive EPS revisions, healthy margins.
narratives.confirmation: Continued beats, rising forward EPS, stable jobless claims.
narratives.invalidation: Earnings misses, negative revisions, labor deterioration.

narratives.name: Higher-for-longer rates pressure multiples
narratives.summary: Sticky inflation keeps policy/real rates elevated, capping valuations.
narratives.evidence: Firm CPI/PCE, hawkish Fed tone, elevated real yields.
narratives.confirmation: Upside inflation surprises, rising 10Y real yield, hawkish dots.
narratives.invalidation: Disinflation resumes, dovish repricing, falling yields.

narratives.name: AI capex and mega-cap leadership
narratives.summary: AI-driven capex and mega-cap earnings carry index returns.
narratives.evidence: Strong mega-cap guidance, rising AI-related capex and revenue.
narratives.confirmation: Continued mega-cap beats and capex follow-through.

narratives.name: Breadth weakness under the index surface
narratives.summary: Narrow leadership masks weakness in the average stock.
narratives.evidence: Cap-weighted outperforming equal-weighted; few stocks above 200-DMA.
narratives.confirmation: Deteriorating A/D, widening cap- vs equal-weight gap.

narratives.name: Inflation reacceleration risk
narratives.summary: A second inflation wave would derail the disinflation/rate-cut path.
narratives.evidence: Sticky services inflation, firming commodities, easy financial conditions.

positioning.summary: Framework: monitor CFTC futures positioning (asset managers vs leveraged funds), dealer gamma (pinning vs accelerant), index put/call activity, the VIX regime, fund flows and systematic exposure (CTA trend, vol-target). Stretched long/gamma-short setups raise air-pocket risk.
positioning.limitations: Exact CFTC COT, dealer-gamma, put/call, fund-flow and systematic-positioning estimates are NOT available in this run. Update from CFTC, options and flow data, then set available=true.

catalysts.whyItMatters: Sets the policy-rate path and risk sentiment; SEP/dots quarterly.
Key inflation gauge driving Fed expectations and yields.
Directly informs the Fed's reaction function.
Anchors the growth/labor and soft-landing read.
catalysts.bearishScenario: Too hot (hawkish) or sharp deterioration (growth scare)

catalysts.whyItMatters: Mega-cap results drive index EPS; quarterly OpEx affects positioning/flows.

confirmationSignals: Break and hold above resistance (PLACEHOLDER level)
Break and hold below support (PLACEHOLDER level)

requirements: Fed on hold with balanced tone
rangeConditions: Price oscillating between PLACEHOLDER support and resistance
rangeBreakSignals: Decisive close beyond the range with follow-through
